meaning: n.艺术院的作风,形成主义
examples: The possibility of something entirely new, the expectation of anti-academicism, flushed into the sclerotic system.
High professional school's cultural construction has the trend of academicism: they refer to comprehensive universities in their purposes enactment, majors setting, and curriculums arrangement.
Incorporating re-created works, exhibitions and installations could have led to a stuffy academicism, but instead it heightens the uncanniness of the anthropomorphized machines.
They were held together by friendship and a wish to be rid of the hobbling academicism of much American poetry in the 1940s.
